explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.findings: Sources reference concerns regarding false and biased AI outputs in the context of fairness practices.
fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

relevant_sources.summary: This article provides evidence for the **governance** and **oversight** pillars of responsible AI by reporting on Constellation Brands' implementation of an AI governance framework. The framework aims to manage AI technology use, and the article also highlights potential risks related to employee and third-party adherence to this framework, indicating a focus on oversight mechanisms. Additionally, the source touches upon the **fairness** pillar by mentioning concerns about "false" and "biased" AI outputs.
relevant_sources.title: ADM and Coca-Cola among Fortune 500 companies citing AI risks

governance.findings: Reports state that the Board of Directors, through its Audit Committee, oversees cybersecurity and enterprise risk management, which implicitly includes AI-related risks. Documents and sources describe the implementation of a governance framework with policies and processes for AI use, which aims to manage AI technology.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This Form 10-K filing provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. The report indicates that the Board of Directors, through its Audit Committee, oversees cybersecurity and enterprise risk management, which implicitly includes AI-related risks. Furthermore, the document mentions the implementation of a governance framework with policies and processes for AI use, suggesting a structured approach to managing AI.
relevant_sources.title: Constellation Brands, Inc. Form 10-K (2024)

relevant_sources.summary: This article provides evidence for the **governance** and **oversight** pillars of responsible AI by reporting on Constellation Brands' implementation of an AI governance framework. The framework aims to manage AI technology use, and the article also highlights potential risks related to employee and third-party adherence to this framework, indicating a focus on oversight mechanisms. Additionally, the source touches upon the **fairness** pillar by mentioning concerns about "false" and "biased" AI outputs.
relevant_sources.title: ADM and Coca-Cola among Fortune 500 companies citing AI risks

oversight.findings: Sources state the implementation of an AI governance framework that aims to manage AI technology use. These sources also describe potential risks related to employee and third-party adherence to this framework, referencing a focus on oversight mechanisms.
oversight.path_to_improvement: Publish override mechanisms, escalation processes, or appeal workflows.

relevant_sources.summary: This article provides evidence for the **governance** and **oversight** pillars of responsible AI by reporting on Constellation Brands' implementation of an AI governance framework. The framework aims to manage AI technology use, and the article also highlights potential risks related to employee and third-party adherence to this framework, indicating a focus on oversight mechanisms. Additionally, the source touches upon the **fairness** pillar by mentioning concerns about "false" and "biased" AI outputs.
relevant_sources.title: ADM and Coca-Cola among Fortune 500 companies citing AI risks

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.path_to_improvement: Document AI systems in use, including vendor/third-party AI tools.

summary.overall_findings: Constellation Brands' published materials state the implementation of an AI governance framework, which aims to manage AI technology use. Additionally, reports indicate that the Board of Directors, through its Audit Committee, oversees enterprise risk management, implicitly including AI-related risks, addressing governance and oversight as 2 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars. Based on 8 publicly available sources, no qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars.
